author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Luke xiii. 6, where we are told of the fig-tree, which bare no fruit for three years; and of the woman which was in an infirmity eighteen years; and on the words of the ninth Psalm, v. 19, ‘Arise, O Lord; let not man prevail: let the nations be judged in thy sight.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xiii. 6, where we are told of the fig-tree, which bare no fruit for three years; and of the woman which was in an infirmity eighteen years; and on the words of the ninth Psalm, v. 19, ‘Arise, O Lord; let not man prevail: let the nations be judged in thy sight.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Touching “the fig-tree” which had its three years’ trial, and bare no fruit, and “the woman which was in an infirmity eighteen years,” hearken to what the Lord may grant me to say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The fig-tree is the human race.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the three years are the three times; one before the Law, the second under the Law, the third under grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now there is nothing unsuitable in understanding by “the fig-tree” the human race.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the first man sinned, he covered his nakedness with fig-leaves; covered those members, from which we derive our birth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what before his sin should have been his glory, after sin became his shame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So before that, “they were naked, and were not ashamed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they had no reason to blush, when no sin had gone before; nor could they blush for their Creator’s works, because they had not yet mingled any evil work of their own with the good works of their Creator.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they had not yet eaten of the tree of knowledge of good and evil, of which they had been forbidden to eat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After then that they had eaten and sinned, the human race sprang from them; that is, man from man, debtor from debtor, mortal from mortal, sinner from sinner.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this “tree” then he entitles those, who through the whole range of time would not bear fruit; and for this cause the axe was hanging over the unfruitful tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The gardener intercedes for it, punishment is deferred, that help may be administered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the gardener who intercedes, is every saint who within the Church prays for those who are without the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what does he pray?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lord, let it alone this year also;” that is, in this time of grace, spare the sinners, spare the unbelievers, spare the barren, spare the unfruitful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will dig about it, and put a basket of dung about it; if it bear fruit, well; but if not, thou shalt come and cut it down.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt come:” When?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou shalt come in judgment, when Thou shalt come to judge the quick and dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Meanwhile they are spared.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what is the “digging”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the “digging about it,” but the teaching lowliness and repentance?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a ditch is low ground.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The basket of dung understand in its good effects.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is filthy, but it produces fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The gardener’s filth is the sinner’s sorrows.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who repent, repent in filthy robes; if, that is, they understand aright, and repent in truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this tree then is it said, “Repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is that “woman who was in an infirmity eighteen years”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In six days God finished His works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Three times six are eighteen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the “three years” then in “the tree” signified, that do the “eighteen years” in this woman.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She was bent down, she could not look up; because in vain did she hear, “Up with your hearts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord made her straight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is hope then, for the children, that is, even until the day of judgment come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man ascribes much to himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet what is man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A righteous man is something great.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But yet a righteous man is righteous only by the grace of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For what is man, save that thou art mindful of him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou see what man is?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All men are liars.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have chanted, “Arise, Lord; let not man prevail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “let not man prevail”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Were not the Apostles men?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Were not Martyrs men?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Himself, without ceasing to be God, vouchsafed to be Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “Arise, Lord; let not man prevail”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If “all men are liars; arise,” Truth, “let not” falsehood “prevail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If man then would be anything good, it must not be of anything of his own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if he should wish to be anything of his own he will be “a liar.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he would wish to be true, he must be so of that which is from God, not of anything of his own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “Arise, Lord; let not man prevail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So much did lying prevail before the flood, that after the flood only eight men remained.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By them the earth was again replenished with lying men, and out of them was elected the people of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Many miracles were wrought, divine benefits imparted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were brought right through to the land of promise, delivered from Egyptian bondage: Prophets were raised up among them, they received the temple, they received the priesthood, they received the anointing, they received the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet of this very people was it said afterwards, “The strange children have lied unto me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At last He was sent who had been promised afore by the Prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let not man prevail,” even the more, because that God was made Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But even He, though He did divine works, was despised, though He showed forth so many acts of mercy, He was apprehended, He was scourged, He was hanged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus far “did man prevail,” to apprehend the Son of God, to scourge the Son of God, to crown the Son of God with thorns, to hang the Son of God upon the tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So far “did man prevail:” how far, but up to the time that having been taken down from the tree, He was laid in the sepulchre?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He had remained there, man would have “prevailed” indeed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this prophecy addresses the very Lord Jesus Himself, saying, “Arise, Lord, let not man prevail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O Lord, Thou hast vouchsafed to come in the flesh, the Word made Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word above us, the Flesh among us, the Word-flesh between God and Man: Thou didst choose a virgin to be born from according to the flesh, when Thou wast to be conceived, Thou didst find a Virgin; when Thou wast born, Thou didst leave a Virgin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But Thou wast not acknowledged; Thou wast Seen, and yet wast hidden.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Infirmity was seen, Power was hidden.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All this was done, that Thou mightest shed that Blood, which is our Price.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou didst so great miracles, didst give health to the weaknesses of the sick, didst show forth many acts of mercy, and receivedst evil for good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They mocked Thee, Thou didst hang upon the tree; the ungodly wagged their heads before Thee, and said, “If Thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hadst Thou then lost Thy power, or rather wast Thou showing forth Thy Patience? and yet they mocked Thee, and yet they derided Thee, yet, when Thou wast slain, they went away as if victorious.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, Thou art laid in the sepulchre: “Arise, Lord, let not man prevail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let not” the ungodly enemy “prevail, let not” the blind Jew “prevail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when Thou wert crucified, the Jew in his blindness seemed to himself to have “prevailed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Arise, Lord, let not man prevail.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is done, yea, it is done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And now what remains, but that “the nations be judged in thy sight”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He hath risen again, as ye know, and ascended into heaven; and from thence He shall come to judge the quick and the dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ah! unfruitful tree, mock not, because thou art yet spared; the axe is delayed, be not thou secure; He will come and thou shalt be cut down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe that He will come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All these things which now ye see, once were not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Once the Christian people were not over the whole world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was read of in prophecy, not seen in the earth; now it is both read and seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus was the Church herself completed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was not said to her, “See, O daughter, and hear;” but, “Hear and see.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the predictions, see the completions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As then, my beloved Brethren, Christ had once not been born of a Virgin, but His birth was promised, and He was born; He had once not done His miracles, they were promised, and He did them: He had not yet suffered, it was promised, and so it came to pass: He had not risen again, it was foretold, and so fulfilled: His Name was not throughout the world, it was foretold, and so fulfilled: the idols were not destroyed and broken down, it was foretold, and so fulfilled: heretics had not assailed the Church, it was foretold, and so fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So also the Day of Judgment is not yet, but seeing it hath been foretold, it shall be fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Can it be that He who in so many things hath shown Himself true, should be false touching the Day of Judgment?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath given us a bond of His promises.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For God hath made Himself a debtor, not by owing ought, that is, not by borrowing; but by promising.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We cannot therefore say to Him, “Give back what Thou hast received.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Since “who hath first given to Him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We cannot say to Him, “Give what Thou hast received;” but we say without scruple, “Give what Thou hast promised.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For hence it is that we are bold to say, day by day, “Thy kingdom come;” that when His kingdom comes, we too may reign with Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Which hath been promised to us in these words; “Then will I say unto them, Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom prepared for you from the beginning of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But assuredly only if we shall have done what follows in that place.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For I was an hungred, and ye gave Me meat,” etc. He made these promises to our fathers; but He hath given us a security, for us too to read.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He who hath vouchsafed to give us this security, were to make a reckoning with us and say, “Read my debts, the debts, that is, of my promises, and reckon up what I have already paid, and reckon also what I still owe; see how many I have paid already; and what I owe is but little; will ye for that little that remains, think Me an untrustworthy promiser?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What should we have to answer against this most evident truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him then who is barren repent, and bear “fruit worthy of repentance.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that is bent down, who looks only on the earth, rejoices in earthly happiness, who thinks this the only happy life, where he may be happy, and who believes no other can be; whosoever he be that is so bent down, let him be made straight; if he cannot by himself, let him call upon God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For was that woman made straight by herself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Woe had it been for her, if He had not stretched out His Hand.
